seoTitle: North Dandalup vs North Fremantle — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between North Dandalup and North Fremantle. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: North Dandalup vs North Fremantle: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Dandalup and North Fremantle.
heading: North Dandalup vs North Fremantle — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Dandalup, Western Australia and North Fremantle, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Fremantle has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to North Dandalup (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Dandalup or North Fremantle safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Dandalup has a flood risk score of 44/100, while North Fremantle scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Dandalup scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. North Fremantle scores 44/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Dandalup or North Fremantle?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Fremantle has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.75% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
